backend/main.mo: Main backend actor for the Raj Empire Esports platform, managing tournaments, player profiles, registrations, support tickets, OTP, social links, terms, and a two-step admin authentication flow using access control and blob storage mixins.
backend/migration.mo: Migration module that upgrades the actor state by adding a nullable `adminPrincipal` field (defaulting to null) to the existing domain/terms/socialLinks state.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (two-step admin auth flow, AdminAuthContext, AdminLoginPage UI) to their source files, components, and test IDs with confidence ratings.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: A JSON document containing short descriptions of each frontend source file, serving as a reference summary for the frontend codebase structure and responsibilities.
frontend/index.css: Base Tailwind CSS file defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark themes, covering background, foreground, card, sidebar, chart, and other UI token colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React app, loading Google Fonts (Rajdhani, Orbitron, Saira Condensed) and mounting the app via `/src/main.tsx`.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that configures TanStack Router with all application routes (public, player-protected, and admin routes), wraps the tree with QueryClient, AuthProvider, and AdminAuthProvider.
frontend/src/components/AdminProtectedRo: Route guard component that redirects unauthenticated users to `/admin` if `isAdminAuthenticated` from AdminAuthContext is false.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Renders the site footer with brand info, quick navigation links, and dynamic social media icons (YouTube, Instagram, Telegram) fetched via a query hook.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: Sticky top navigation bar with logo, desktop/mobile nav links, and conditional auth controls (login/register or dashboard/logout) based on authentication state.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Root layout component that wraps all pages with a shared Header and Footer, rendering child routes via an Outlet.
frontend/src/components/PaymentModal.tsx: Modal for tournament registration that lets players upload a payment screenshot, displays QR code and entry fee info, and submits registration via a mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.t: Route guard component that redirects unauthenticated users to the login page and renders children only when authenticated.
frontend/src/context/AdminAuthContext.ts: Provides admin authentication context requiring both credential verification (hardcoded username/password) and Internet Identity verification, with login/logout state persisted in localStorage.
frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx: Provides player authentication context with session stored in localStorage, exposing login/logout functions and the current player session.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using the current Internet Identity, returning an anonymous actor when unauthenticated and invalidating dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication using DFINITY's AuthClient, exposing login/logout functions, identity state, and login status.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching and mutating backend data including tournaments, registrations, players, support tickets, social links, and terms and conditions.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports Google Fonts, configures Tailwind CSS with custom CSS variables for light/dark themes, and defines brand gradient and scrollbar utilities.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that renders the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboardPage.ts: Admin dashboard page with a sidebar navigation for managing tournaments, registrations, players, terms, support tickets, and social links using various query/mutation hooks.
frontend/src/pages/AdminLoginPage.tsx: Two-step admin login page requiring credential verification followed by Internet Identity authentication, with appropriate loading and error states.
frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx: Player dashboard page displaying tabbed views for browsing tournaments, viewing personal registrations, and accessing profile information.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: Landing page for Raj Empire Esports Arena featuring a hero section with CTA buttons to view tournaments or register, and a features grid highlighting daily tournaments, mega events, team modes, and fair play.
frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx: Two-step login page that collects a mobile number, generates and verifies an OTP, then fetches the user profile to authenticate and redirect to the player dashboard.
frontend/src/pages/RegisterPage.tsx: Three-step registration page that collects display name, mobile, and BGMI Player ID, verifies via OTP, registers the player on-chain, saves the profile, and logs the user in.
frontend/src/pages/TermsPage.tsx: Displays the platform's Terms & Conditions by fetching them from the backend (with a hardcoded fallback), parsing numbered sections, and rendering each with styled headings and colored left borders.
frontend/src/pages/TournamentsPage.tsx: Lists all BGMI tournaments with search and status filter controls; unauthenticated users are redirected to login on register attempt, while authenticated users get a PaymentModal for tournament registration.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, storing, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routes, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom fonts, OKLCH-based color tokens, brand colors/gradients, shadows, border radii, and accordion animations for the frontend.
project_state.json: Project state manifest describing the esports tournament platform's architecture, existing features, and admin two-step authentication flow (credentials + Internet Identity).
